data.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq rise, Dow lags after Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extension
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were mixed on Friday as investors digested the latest developments in the Middle East, including a three-week extension to the truce between Israel and Lebanon.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.4%, while the heavy-tech Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1%. Meanwhile, the blue-chip Dow was -0.4%.

Treasury yields were lower. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 2 basis points lower at 4.31%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) dropped 6 basis points to 3.79%.

U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon will be extended for another three weeks after hosting ambassadors of both countries in Washington. 

"The meeting went very well! The United States is going to work with Lebanon in order to help it protect itself from Hezbollah. The ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon will be extended by THREE WEEKS," he said via Truth Social.

Trump also ordered the U.S. Navy to “shoot and kill” any vessel found placing mines in the Strait of Hormuz, adding that there will be "no hesitation."

Moreover, Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps navy laid more mines in the Strait of Hormuz this week, according to a report by Axios.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) stayed higher at $95, while Brent (CO1:COM) was last around $105 per barrel.

On the economy, the Consumer Sentiment Index was revised up in April—to 49.8 from the initial 47.6 and 53.3 in March—according to the University of Michigan Survey of Consumers.

data.title: Bets on when Warsh will be confirmed as Fed jumped as DOJ announced drop of criminal probe against Powell
data.description:
Bets on when Kevin Warsh will be confirmed as Fed Chair spiked after the news that the Department of Justice is dropping the criminal probe of current Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

According to predictions market Kalshi (KALSHI), bets that Warsh will be confirmed before June 1 jumped to 98.5%—up from about 60% earlier on Friday—while bets that he will be confirmed before May 15 are now at 84%, up from about 31% on Thursday.

 

 

 

The Department of Justice will drop its criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, ending a standoff that threatened to delay the confirmation of Powell’s successor, according to District of Columbia U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro on Friday.

The probe, which centered on alleged cost overruns at the Fed’s $2.5B Washington headquarters renovation project, will be referred to the Federal Reserve’s independent inspector general for review.

The closure resolves a legal battle that included D.C. District Judge James Boasberg’s ruling last month tossing out subpoenas Pirro had sent to Powell. Despite the investigation’s end, Pirro made clear the matter is not entirely settled.

“Note well, however, that I will not hesitate to restart a criminal investigation should the facts warrant doing so,” he said.

Powell, whose term ends next month, had previously revealed the investigation in January and called it an attempt by the Trump administration to pressure the Fed to lower interest rates.

More on the Federal Reserve

  • Fed chair nominee Warsh's inflation strategy could backfire - BofA
  • U.S. SEC, Treasury seek more data on private credit risks - report
  • Trump keeps pressure on Powell as Warsh faces lawmakers
  • DOJ closes Powell probe as Fed watchdog takes over cost review
  • Fed chair nominee seeks policy reforms, new framework, new communications

data.title: Charter Communications shares slump on Q1 results, dragging down sector peers
data.description:
Shares of Charter Communications (CHTR) are facing heavy selling pressure on Friday following the release of mixed first-quarter results that included a decline in revenue and a larger-than-expected loss of broadband customers.

The parent company of Spectrum mobile and internet services added 368K new mobile lines (versus +432K estimates) during the quarter but lost 120K Spectrum Internet customers (versus -100K estimates) amid increased promotions from its competitors.

And although its video service lost 60K customers, it was less than the 85K decline analysts were expecting and compares to a loss of 181K customers in the same quarter last year. This can be attributed to Spectrum’s simplified pricing and packaging, along with the inclusion of programmers’ streaming applications in its expanded basic video packages.

For the first quarter, Charter Communications (CHTR) earned a profit of $9.17 per share in the first quarter, up 9% from a year ago but $0.91 below expectations. Adjusted EBITDA declined 2.2% to $5.6B, compressing adjusted EBITDA margin by 50 basis points to 41.5%.

On the revenue side, a 15.1% increase in mobile service revenue and advertising revenue was unable to fully compensate for losses in internet, video, voice, and residential service, all of which contributed to a 1.1% decline in revenue to a below-consensus $13.5B. 

The company’s balance sheet is also weighing on shares as free cash flow decreased 12.5% to $1.4B.

In response to the disappointing results, Charter Communications CEO Chris Winfrey assured investors that “as we continue to improve our products, pricing, packaging, and service, and complete our rural network initiatives, we are poised for improving customer and free cash flow growth.”

The results are weighing on peers within the sector, driving down shares of Comcast (CMCSA), Liberty Broadband Corp. (LBRDK) (LBRDA), Optimum Communications (OPTU), and Cable One (CABO)

data.title: COMPASS Pathways psilocybin asset for depression awarded Commissioner voucher
data.description:
• COMP360, a synthetic psilocybin-based drug for treatment-resistant depression from COMPASS Pathways (CMPS), is the latest therapy to receive an FDA Commissioner's National Priority Voucher.
  • As a result, the candidate will have a review time of one to two months once an NDA is filed, as well as improved communications with the agency.
  • The FDA has also granted the company's request for a rolling submission. 
  • In February, COMPASS Pathways announced a late-stage trial of COMP360 reached the primary endpoint.

data.title: OpenAI's launch of GPT-5.5 is indicative of the ongoing frontier model race: HSBC
data.description:
This week's announcement by OpenAI (OPENAI) of its latest model, GPT-5.5, is indicative of the ongoing frontier model race, investment firm HSBC said.

“LLMs are engaged in a race to delivering the most intelligent platform,” analysts at the firm wrote in a note to clients. “Leading positions are unstable, with the latest iteration usually beating older frontier models.”

Nearly a year ago, Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini was thought to be a laggard but is now considered one of the leaders, HSBC added. The same goes for Meta (META) after it released Muse Spark earlier this month, which is now competitive against Anthropic (ANTHRO) Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1, or GPT-5.4. And given the fact that Western companies are focused on reaching artificial general intelligence, the race is unlikely to slow down anytime soon, HSBC added.

“In Asia, we see a trend for open source, lighter and cheaper models that have the capacity to penetrate production processes rapidly (DeepSeek just launched its V4),” HSBC added. “In the West, given the high sunk costs, only a handful of models may have the capacity to pursue AGI. The most likely outcome for the market structure could be an oligopoly, yielding good returns in the long run. But in the meantime, massive compute power is required, leading to funding gaps.”

data.title: Financial Institutions targets 5% loan growth and full-year net interest margin in the upper 360s
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Financial Institutions, Inc. (FISI) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "Our first quarter results underscore the strength of our community banking franchise, reflecting disciplined execution by our team and a continued focus on sustainable profitability." (President, CEO & Director Martin Birmingham)
  • "We delivered net income available to common shareholders of $20.6 million or $1.04 per diluted share" and highlighted "return on average assets of 137 basis points" and "an efficiency ratio of 57%." (President, CEO & Director Birmingham)
  • "In January, we completed the refinancing of $65 million of legacy sub-debt issuances" and "we repurchased a little over 163,000 shares" with "the total repurchase since December" at "approximately 500,000 shares or half the 5% authorization"; the Board also approved "a 3.2% increase in our quarterly cash dividend to $0.32 per common share." (President, CEO & Director Birmingham)
  • "Based on the size and health of the pipelines we have today, we expect to see loan growth rebound through the second half of the year and continue to expect full year loan growth of 5%, driven by commercial." (President, CEO & Director Birmingham)
  • "We offboarded the remaining $7 million of BaaS-related deposits in the first quarter, marking the completion of our Banking-as-a-Service wind-down" and reported "Period-end total deposits were $5.34 billion"; management also said it continues to "target low single-digit deposit growth for the full year." (President, CEO & Director Birmingham)
  • "Our business lines came together to achieve profitable financial performance in the first quarter, highlighted by NIM expansion, durability of key noninterest income categories and disciplined expense management." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Jack Plants)

Outlook

  • "We still anticipate modest incremental NIM expansion for the rest of the year and now expect to achieve full year net interest margin in the upper 360s" and added, "our guidance is based on a spot rate forecast, which does not factor in potential future rate cuts." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)
  • "Given our favorable first quarter results, we now expect to deliver a full year efficiency ratio approaching 57%." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)
  • "The 2026 effective tax rate is now expected to be at the lower end of our guided range of between 16.5% to 17.5%." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)
  • "We expect to remain within our previously disclosed full year charge-off guidance of 25 to 35 basis points." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)

Financial Results

  • "We delivered net income available to common shareholders of $20.6 million or $1.04 per diluted share." (President, CEO & Director Birmingham)
  • "The 367 basis point NIM we reported for the first quarter was stronger than we anticipated due to favorable deposit pricing." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)
  • "Noninterest income was $10.7 million" and "associated swap fee income was $239,000, as compared to $1.1 million in Q4." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)
  • "We reported quarterly noninterest expense of $35.6 million, down from $36.7 million in the fourth quarter." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)
  • "Net charge-offs were 44 basis points of average loans, compared to 21 basis points in the linked-quarter" and "Our allowance for credit losses was 97 basis points of total loans this quarter." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)

Q&A

  • Damon Del Monte, KBW: asked about maintaining "this upper 360s level" for margin; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants: "the cost of interest-bearing liabilities ended March at 2.49%" and said "we are approaching the bottom from a cost of funds perspective," while adding that "the spreads that we're recognizing on originations" should support "margin stability through the rest of the year." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)
  • Damon Del Monte, KBW: asked about "back book repricing"; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants: "we have about $1 billion on a rolling 12-month basis of cash flow that comes off the loan portfolio" and said "incremental improvement in new origination yields versus what's running off" is "driving some of that earning asset yield benefit." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)
  • Damon Del Monte, KBW: asked about remaining buyback capacity; President, CEO & Director Birmingham: "Number one is our CET ratio, CET1, and really a floor of 11%," and also "ensuring we've got capacity to support growth," adding, "the earn-back is at or around a year." (President, CEO & Director Birmingham)
  • [Ekyu Nazir], on behalf of Piper Sandler: asked how the company plans to maintain the "5% guide" and requested pipeline detail; President, CEO & Director Birmingham: "the pipeline currently stands at almost $1 billion, $950 million-ish," with "C&I pipeline activities" about "2x" historical levels and "CRE opportunities" at "a little over $600 million"; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants: added that construction commitments "planned to draw down" are "not represented in the $1 billion loan pipeline." (President, CEO & Director Birmingham; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)
  • [Ekyu Nazir], on behalf of Piper Sandler: asked about loan and deposit pricing competition; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants: "we are seeing the market being quite competitive on deposit rates" and said commercial loan pricing is "as competitive as it has been," but "spreads that we've observed have been within our tolerances." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was neutral to slightly skeptical, with repeated probing on sustainability of margin, loan-growth visibility, and competitive deposit pricing, including Del Monte asking what "give you confidence" on margin and Piper Sandler asking how the company plans to "rebound" to maintain the loan-growth guide. (Damon Del Monte, KBW; [Ekyu Nazir], on behalf of Piper Sandler)
  • Management tone was slightly positive and explanatory, emphasizing confidence and guardrails, including "we are approaching the bottom from a cost of funds perspective" and "we're very confident in our ability to achieve that 5% target," while framing buybacks around an "11%" CET1 floor. (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants; President, CEO & Director Birmingham)
  • Compared with Q4 2025, guidance language tightened upward in places as management said it "now expect[s] to achieve full year net interest margin in the upper 360s" versus the prior "mid-360s" target, while continuing to reference a spot-rate framework that "does not factor in potential future rate cuts." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Profitability messaging shifted from Q4’s full-year framing ("$73.4 million or $3.61 per diluted share" for 2025) to Q1’s emphasis on quarterly profitability and capital actions, including "$20.6 million or $1.04 per diluted share" and half of the "5% authorization" already repurchased. (President, CEO & Director Birmingham)
  • Net interest margin outlook moved higher as Q4 guidance was "a full year target in the mid-360s" and Q1 updated to "full year net interest margin in the upper 360s," with Q1 attributing upside to "favorable deposit pricing." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)
  • Commercial loan growth expectations were consistent in direction but more data-driven in Q1, with Q4 noting growth would be "lighter in Q1 and build through the year" and Q1 citing Q1 "originations were $147 million with $158 million in payoffs and paydowns" and a pipeline of "$950 million-ish." (President, CEO & Director Birmingham)
  • Banking-as-a-Service wind-down moved from nearing completion in Q4 ("approximately $7 million" expected to roll off in Q1) to completion in Q1 ("offboarded the remaining $7 million" and "took BaaS deposits to 0"). (President, CEO & Director Birmingham)

Risks and Concerns

  • Management pointed to "geopolitical and economic uncertainty" as a driver of customers "tightening their balance sheets and paying down debt with cash reserves," affecting "lower loans and deposits." (President, CEO & Director Birmingham)
  • Deposit competition remained a central risk theme as management said it continues to see "competitive pressure on deposit pricing" and is emphasizing "primary customer relationships" that "may modestly impact our cost of funds." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)
  • Credit costs were flagged as higher in the quarter, with "net charge-offs" at "44 basis points," while management said the quarter included "a previously disclosed commercial business relationship" that had been "fully reserved for in a prior year" and reaffirmed full-year charge-off guidance. (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Plants)

Final Takeaway

Management described Q1 as a quarter of stronger-than-anticipated margin and continued expense discipline, while keeping full-year priorities centered on "full year loan growth of 5%, driven by commercial," "full year net interest margin in the upper 360s," and capital return actions supported by an "11%" CET1 floor. They also positioned the completion of the BaaS deposit wind-down, improving loan pipelines ("$950 million-ish"), and relationship-based deposit strategy as key execution points amid ongoing competitive deposit pricing and macro uncertainty.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: PagerDuty gains amid activist investor speculation
data.description:
PagerDuty (PD) rose 9.3% amid speculation it may be the target of an activist investor.

Goldman Sachs filed a 13G on Thursday, disclosing a 4.32 million stake in Q1, up from ~2 million at the end of 4Q, and Goldman is the company's fourth-biggest holder. Goldman can sometimes be used as a counterparty for activist derivative positions, Dealreporter highlighted in an item on Friday.

A potential activist in PagerDuty (PD) comes as shares of the company have plunged more than 60% over the past year. Last July, Reuters reported that PagerDuty was exploring options, including a sale, after seeing inbound interest. 

Takeover interest is now new to PagerDuty (NYSE:PD). In January 2024, it was reported that the software maker was evaluating its options amid  interest from private equity firms.

data.title: Delinquencies, charge-offs drop as consumers pull back on borrowing: March Credit Pulse
data.description:
U.S. credit card delinquencies and charge-offs dropped month-over-month across major banks in the prior month, according to Seeking Alpha's March Credit Pulse.

Seeking Alpha tracks delinquency and charge-off numbers for seven banks in its monthly roundup: American Express (AXP), Synchrony Financial (SYF), Citigroup (C), Bread Financial (BFH), Capital One (COF), JPMorgan Chase (JPM), and Bank of America (BAC).

Average delinquency rate dropped to 2.70% from 2.81% in February. Furthermore, the metric was below the March 2025 average of 2.84%.

Notably, the March delinquency rate came below the pre-pandemic level of 2.76% (March 2019) and the three-month average of 2.77%.

Average net charge-off rate for March came in at 3.38%, down from 3.83% in February and 4.34% in March 2025. The pre-pandemic rate was 4.05%, while the three-month average was 3.58%.

Households are pulling back on borrowing and reducing revolving balances, leading to improvements in consumer credit conditions, showed the latest CreditGauge edition from VantageScore.

Average credit card balances and utilization rates declined month-over-month in March, according to the credit scoring company.

"The decline in revolving balances provides clearer evidence of consumer deleveraging, suggesting households are paying down debt and moderating spending, supported in part by seasonal inflows such as tax refunds," said the report.

Here is a look at the March delinquencies and net charge-off rates:

data.title: DraftKings and Flutter see ratings cuts at MoffettNathanson due to prediction market overhang
data.description:
DraftKings (DKNG) and Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) were cut at MoffettNathanson to a Neutral rating from Buy.

While the firm has a core belief that the valuation of both companies is attractive, even on conservative longer-term forecasts, a major overhang is seen on both sports betting stocks  until there is some regulatory clarity on the prediction markets industry.

Currently, the U.S. prediction market is sitting in a rapidly evolving but still unsettled regulatory environment. The CFTC has moved toward a formal framework, including a March 12 advance notice of proposed rulemaking and a staff advisory, while also withdrawing an earlier proposal that could have tightened limits on sports- and politics-related contracts.

At the same time, state regulators and litigants are aggressively challenging the industry, especially where contracts overlap with gambling or sports betting. The result today is  a patchwork of federal oversight, active rulemaking, and ongoing state-level legal pressure.

DraftKings (DKNG) and Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) are both down more than 30% since the beginning of the year as prediction market trading volume continues to exceed expectations for major sporting events.
